headline: Prediction Markets Emerge in Japan, With Shopping Vouchers
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Even as prediction platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi surged into the global mainstream in recent years, Japan — like its Asian neighbors China and South Korea — remained a no-go zone due to strict anti-gambling regulations. That may be starting to change, thanks to enterprising new startups that are finding ways to skirt regulations.Leading the charge are homegrown platforms launched by Gen Z entrepreneurs. headline: Mizuho says ignore Apple noise, flags key catalyst for Micron and memory in H2
summary: The first bear catalyst is a report that Apple is seeking U.S. government approval to source DRAM from Chinese chipmaker CXMT, which appears on the Commerce Department's Entity List. Apple's interest in Chinese memory is a symptom of industry-wide scarcity, not a Micron-specific problem.

content.title: Mizuho says ignore Apple noise, flags key catalyst for Micron and memory in H2
content.summary: The first bear catalyst is a report that Apple is seeking U.S. government approval to source DRAM from Chinese chipmaker CXMT, which appears on the Commerce Department's Entity List. Apple's interest in Chinese memory is a symptom of industry-wide scarcity, not a Micron-specific problem.

headline: Wall Street Weans Itself From Tech in Early Summer Rotation
summary: The stock market is broadening beyond tech as investors look for new market leadership into the summer months and over the back half of the year.  Healthcare, industrials, and financials are the market’s three leading sectors over the past month as investors execute a quiet rotation out of tech and artificial intelligence names.

headline: Comcast, Alphabet, Palantir, SpaceX, Microsoft, and More Stocks That Explain Today’s Market
summary: Technology stocks were trading higher in premarket trading Monday as the  looked set to finally snap its five-day losing streak.  Semiconductor companies, other artificial-intelligence stocks, and the Magnificent Seven were among those rebounding to start the week.  Microsoft  jumped 2% ahead of the open, while  Amazon Alphabet Nvidia Meta  and  Tesla  were all up around 1%.

headline: Exclusive-Apple accuses India of 'copy-pasting' rivals' claims in antitrust investigation
summary: Apple has accused Indian antitrust investigators of "copy-pasting" its rivals' claims and failing to properly conduct its own investigation in concluding the U.S. tech giant breached competition laws, calling for the findings to be quashed, regulatory papers reviewed by Reuters showed.  The June 25 Apple submission, being ‌reported for the first time, marks the sharpest escalation yet in Apple's fight with the Competition Commission of India (CCI), where Tinder-owner Match and Indian startups are among its ‌opponents.  In 2024, CCI investigators privately issued a report saying Apple engaged in "abusive conduct" on the apps platform of its iOS operating system, and wrongly mandated the use of its payment system.

content.title: Exclusive-Apple accuses India of 'copy-pasting' rivals' claims in antitrust investigation
content.summary: Apple has accused Indian antitrust investigators of "copy-pasting" its rivals' claims and failing to properly conduct its own investigation in concluding the U.S. tech giant breached competition laws, calling for the findings to be quashed, regulatory papers reviewed by Reuters showed.  The June 25 Apple submission, being ‌reported for the first time, marks the sharpest escalation yet in Apple's fight with the Competition Commission of India (CCI), where Tinder-owner Match and Indian startups are among its ‌opponents.  In 2024, CCI investigators privately issued a report saying Apple engaged in "abusive conduct" on the apps platform of its iOS operating system, and wrongly mandated the use of its payment system.
